name: Indium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Indium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Indium oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption in Indium oxide layer

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise oxide removal

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for Indium oxide ablation

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled oxide ablation

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete oxide removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform coverage

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in metal cleaning; lower end for delicate soft metals like Indium to avoid melting (mp 156.6°C). Recommended start conservatively low. Source: IPG Photonics specs for cleaning heads; conservative for Indium's low thermal resistance (similar to Al studies in Opt. Lasers Eng. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal on reflective metals; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light. Recommended balances speed and fluence control. Source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals; adapted for Indium's high reflectivity reducing absorption.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with minimal HAZ on soft metals; picosecond (ps) for ultra-precision but overkill for Indium. Avoids excessive heat buildup due to Indium's conductivity (82 W/m·K). Source: Laser physics in J. Appl. Phys. 2015 on ns ablation thresholds for low-mp metals.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for industrial cleaning; moderate rate prevents heat accumulation on heat-sensitive Indium while enabling efficient coverage. Higher rates increase speed but risk thermal damage. Source: Academic lit. in Appl. Surf. Sci. 2020 on pulse train effects for metals.

energy_density.description: Fluence below Indium's ablation threshold (~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to reflectivity >90%); safe for contaminant removal without substrate melting. Conservative to account for variability in contamination. Source: Ablation studies in Phys. Procedia 2013 for soft metals; estimated for Indium.

spot_size.description: Smaller for precise surface prep on Indium components (e.g., electronics); larger for broad areas. Balances fluence and coverage. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent Inc. cleaning systems; suitable for fine Indium features.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass for thorough removal of layered contamination without single-pass overload; single pass for light cleaning. Recommended for uniform Indium surface prep. Source: Industry case studies in J. Laser Appl. 2019 on metal oxide removal.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular Indium surfaces; 50% optimal for efficiency without excessive exposure. Higher for heavy contamination. Source: Process optimization in Opt. Laser Technol. 2017 for scanned laser cleaning.
